table.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top;

tr.style: border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: The logical name of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Information about the use of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Minimum and Maximum # of times the element can appear in the instance

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px; width: 100px

a.title: Reference to the type of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck1.png)
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck13.png)

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck124.png)
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td:
Original or display-oriented textual representation of the full quantity
URL: http://314e.com/fhir/StructureDefinition/quantity-quantityString

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck01.png)

a.title:
The value of the measured amount.
Whenever precision is needed, precision metadata SHALL be explicitly represented using the quantityPrecision extension on the 314e decimal profile. The quantityPrecision extension represents the number of significant decimal places intended for the quantity value, irrespective of how many decimal places are explicitly present in the decimal representation itself. The presence of this extension does not alter the underlying numeric value or computation semantics.
The original textual representation of the full quantity may be preserved using the valueString extension.
The original textual representation of just the quantity value may additionally be preserved using the valueString extension.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: Numerical value with explicit precision support

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck003.png)

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck0024.png)
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td:
Original or display-oriented textual representation of the value
URL: http://314e.com/fhir/StructureDefinition/quantity-valueString

description.value: A 314e-constrained SimpleQuantity datatype supporting precision metadata and alternate textual representations.

definition.value: The comparator is not used on a SimpleQuantity

comment.value: The context of use may frequently define what kind of quantity this is and therefore what kind of units can be used. The context of use may also restrict the values for the comparator.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
If a code for the unit is present, the system SHALL also be present
The comparator is not used on a SimpleQuantity

map.value: PQ, IVL<PQ>, MO, CO, depending on the values

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

description.value: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Original or display-oriented textual representation of the full quantity

definition.value: Provides the original, source-oriented, or display-oriented textual representation of the complete quantity, including value and unit.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Numerical value with explicit precision support

definition.value:
The value of the measured amount.
Whenever precision is needed, precision metadata SHALL be explicitly represented using the quantityPrecision extension on the 314e decimal profile. The quantityPrecision extension represents the number of significant decimal places intended for the quantity value, irrespective of how many decimal places are explicitly present in the decimal representation itself. The presence of this extension does not alter the underlying numeric value or computation semantics.
The original textual representation of the full quantity may be preserved using the valueString extension.
The original textual representation of just the quantity value may additionally be preserved using the valueString extension.

comment.value: The implicit precision in the value should always be honored. Monetary values have their own rules for handling precision (refer to standard accounting text books).

requirements.value: Precision is handled implicitly in almost all cases of measurement.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: PQ.value, CO.value, MO.value, IVL.high or IVL.low depending on the value

definition.value: unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references)

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Explicit precision of the decimal value

definition.value:
Explicit precision of the number.
This represents the intended or known number of significant decimal places irrespective of how many are explicitly represented in the decimal value itself.

comment.value: Applications performing calculations SHALL ensure that this extension is updated appropriately if the numeric value changes.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Original or display-oriented textual representation of the value

definition.value: Original textual representation of the quantity value as received from the source system.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

definition.value: Not allowed to be used in this context

requirements.value: Need a framework for handling measures where the value is <5ug/L or >400mg/L due to the limitations of measuring methodology.

meaningWhenMissing.value: If there is no comparator, then there is no modification of the value

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: This is labeled as "Is Modifier" because the comparator modifies the interpretation of the value significantly. If there is no comparator, then there is no modification of the value

description.value: How the Quantity should be understood and represented.

definition.value: A human-readable form of the unit.

requirements.value: There are many representations for units of measure and in many contexts, particular representations are fixed and required. I.e. mcg for micrograms.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: System that defines coded unit form

definition.value: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

requirements.value: Need to know the system that defines the coded form of the unit.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

comment.value: The preferred system is UCUM, but SNOMED CT can also be used (for customary units) or ISO 4217 for currency. The context of use may additionally require a code from a particular system.

requirements.value: Need a computable form of the unit that is fixed across all forms. UCUM provides this for quantities, but SNOMED CT provides many units of interest.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Original or display-oriented textual representation of the full quantity

definition.value: Provides the original, source-oriented, or display-oriented textual representation of the complete quantity, including value and unit.

short.value: Numerical value with explicit precision support

definition.value:
The value of the measured amount.
Whenever precision is needed, precision metadata SHALL be explicitly represented using the quantityPrecision extension on the 314e decimal profile. The quantityPrecision extension represents the number of significant decimal places intended for the quantity value, irrespective of how many decimal places are explicitly present in the decimal representation itself. The presence of this extension does not alter the underlying numeric value or computation semantics.
The original textual representation of the full quantity may be preserved using the valueString extension.
The original textual representation of just the quantity value may additionally be preserved using the valueString extension.

short.value: Original or display-oriented textual representation of the value

definition.value: Original textual representation of the quantity value as received from the source system.
